page_content:
Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in BSCI202.

Recommended: BSCI223.

Restriction: Must be in Public Health Science program; and junior standing or higher.

Credit Only Granted for. PHSC415, SPHL415 or SPHL498J.

Formerly: SPHL415 and SPHL498J.

PHSC420 Vaccines and Immunology (3 Credits)

An exploration of immunology and vaccines through a public health lens. We will examine the cells, systems, and molecules that comprise the human immune system and defend your body against disease. In addition, we will discuss the strategies used during vaccine development including the history and future of vaccination and how increased understanding of the immune system has allowed scientists to improve and refine the process. Finally we will examine the current social

and political situation surrounding vaccination and the roles and responsibility of public health practitioners.
